name: Pasta con Asparagi e Pancetta
method: In a large frying pan on medium heat, add 1 T olive oil and 1 T butter, chop up and add parboiled asparagus with white stalks and tips removed. Set aside tips for later. Parboil means cooking the asparagus halfway by boiling it in water. Add onion, salt, and pepper, and fry for a few minutes. Remove from heat. In a blender, add the ingredients you just fried, and cream. Blend for 1-2 minutes. Set aside. Don't wash the frying pan because we are going to use it later. Boil a large pot of salted water, add pasta, pictured is farfalle, but you can use another kind of pasta if you like.

In the frying pan you used to fry the asparagus, on medium heat, add 1 T butter, 1 T olive oil, and fry the pancetta until crispy. Add asparagus tips and fry one minute. When pasta is ready, add directly to the frying pan with fried pancetta, and mix well. Add the blended asparagus-cream mixture on top, then grated pecorino or grana, add 1-2 ladles of pasta water, and mix well, then manticare to cover all sides of the pasta with creamy sauce. Garnish with grated parmesan and asparagus tips. Serves 2-3. Serving suggestion: fresh bread and salad. Olé
